global.title: Alan Greenspan dies at 100 as markets reassess the economic moment
global.summary: Former U.S. Federal Reserve chair Alan Greenspan has died at 100, with the news landing as investors weigh higher-rate expectations and renewed market volatility.
global.whyItMatters: A major public figure’s death invites reflection on stewardship, economic influence, and the anxieties many families feel around money.

global.title: More than 11,000 seafarers begin exiting the Persian Gulf under backed transit plan
global.summary: The International Maritime Organization says stranded seafarers will begin leaving through the Strait of Hormuz under a plan supported by both the U.S. and Iran, a sign of practical de-escalation in the region.
global.whyItMatters: This is a concrete answer to prayer for peace and protection of vulnerable workers caught in geopolitical tension.

global.title: UK markets steady after Keir Starmer resignation shifts political focus
global.summary: Reuters reports British markets remained relatively calm after Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s resignation, with attention now turning to succession and fiscal leadership.
global.whyItMatters: Leadership transitions in major allies affect public confidence and offer a timely lens on character, stability, and service under pressure.

canadian.title: Regina mosque faces threats after launching weekly amplified call to prayer
canadian.summary: Google News highlights threats against a Regina mosque and increased police patrols around Muslim gathering places following the launch of a weekly amplified call to prayer.
canadian.whyItMatters: Pastors should notice rising religious hostility in Canada and be ready to speak clearly for neighbor-love and religious freedom for all.

canadian.title: New N.W.T. highway expected to become Carney government’s first national-interest project
canadian.summary: Google News reports a new Northwest Territories highway is expected to be the federal government’s first designated national-interest project.
canadian.whyItMatters: Infrastructure decisions shape regional access, Indigenous relationships, and the economic pressures facing northern communities.

local.title: Man charged with manslaughter after Abbotsford homeless encampment shooting
local.summary: CBC’s B.C. roundup reports a manslaughter charge has been laid in connection with a shooting at a homeless encampment in Abbotsford.
local.whyItMatters: This touches local vulnerability, public safety, and the church’s calling to care for people living on the margins.

local.title: Canucks bringing development camp to Abbotsford
local.summary: The Chilliwack Progress reports the Vancouver Canucks are bringing a development camp to Abbotsford, creating a notable community sports moment in the Fraser Valley.
local.whyItMatters: Shared local events can become bridges for community connection, outreach, and conversations with families.

local.title: Pilot program in Abbotsford, Chilliwack, and Mission exceeds original target
local.summary: Fraser Valley Current reports a pilot program serving Abbotsford, Chilliwack, and Mission has already surpassed its initial goal.
local.whyItMatters: It is worth watching signs of local momentum and practical problem-solving that may open doors for partnership and encouragement.

persecutedChurch.title: Jerusalem’s Latin and Greek patriarchs visit Gaza and vow not to abandon its Christians
persecutedChurch.summary: The Times of Israel reports that Jerusalem’s Latin and Greek patriarchs visited Gaza and publicly pledged continued presence and support for the Christian community there.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a meaningful sign of pastoral solidarity with a fragile church under extreme pressure and a prompt for prayerful remembrance.

persecutedChurch.title: Germany records highest-ever number of antisemitic incidents in 2025
persecutedChurch.summary: The New York Post, citing a German monitoring report, says antisemitic incidents reached a record 8,725 in 2025, including assaults, threats, and extreme violence.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Hatred toward religious minorities is intensifying in the West, and pastors should be alert to how fear and scapegoating spread through societies.

leadership.title: Fitness and durability are becoming part of the leadership conversation
leadership.summary: Forbes argues that modern leadership is shifting away from glorifying exhaustion and toward habits of sleep, exercise, nutrition, and resilience that sustain long-term service.
leadership.whyItMatters: For a pastor, this is a useful reminder that faithful leadership requires stewardship of body, mind, and endurance.

goodNews.title: Story hour helps war-affected children simply be children again
goodNews.summary: Ynetnews tells how a Miami speech therapist created a free Hebrew-English Zoom storytime after Oct. 7, giving children in Israel routine, comfort, and connection across borders.
goodNews.whyItMatters: This is a hopeful picture of small, faithful care meeting deep need—an encouraging model for ministry in anxious times.
